name: Algebraic analysis of order
statement_text:
Let M = (A, U, B, V ) denote a general linear method and
let ξ denote the algebraic representation of a starting method S. Assume that
(532e) and (532f) hold in G/Hp . Denote

                         = Eξ − BηD − V ξ,          in G.

Then the Taylor expansion of S(y(x0 + h)) − M (S(y(x0 ))) is
                                   (t) r(t)
                                        h F (t)(y(x0 )).                 (532g)
                                   σ(t)
                          r(t)>p
proof_text:
Proof. We consider a single step from initial data given at x0 and consider the
Taylor expansion of various expressions about x0 . The input approximation,
computed by S, has Taylor series represented by ξ. Suppose the Taylor
expansions for the stage values are represented by η so that the stage
derivatives will be represented by ηD and these will be related by (532e). The
Taylor expansion for the output approximations is represented by BηD + V ξ,
and this will agree with the Taylor expansion of S(y(x0 + h)) up to hp terms
if (532f) holds. The difference from the target value of S(y(x0 + h)) is given
by (532g).

variables.name: M
variables.definition: M = (A, U, B, V) denotes a general linear method

variables.name: ξ
variables.definition: ξ is the algebraic representation of the starting method S

variables.name: η
variables.definition: η is a vector defined by η = AηD + Uξ in G/Hp

variables.name: E
variables.definition: E is an operator (likely related to shifting or advancing the method)

variables.name: G
variables.definition: G is a space (likely of functions or series)

variables.name: Hp
variables.definition: Hp is a subspace of G, with G/Hp denoting a quotient space

variables.name: p
variables.definition: p is an integer related to the order of truncation or the space Hp

variables.name: r(t)
variables.definition: r(t) is a function (likely the order of a tree t) used in Taylor expansions

variables.name: σ(t)
variables.definition: σ(t) is a symmetry coefficient for a tree t

variables.name: F(t)
variables.definition: F(t) is an elementary differential associated with a tree t

equations.content: η = AηD + Uξ, interpreted in G/Hp
Eξ = BηD + Vξ, interpreted in G/Hp

preamble: The theorem concerns a general linear method M with algebraic representation ξ of a starting method S. Equations (532e) and (532f) hold in the quotient space G/Hp, where p relates to truncation order. The expression Eξ - BηD - Vξ in G represents a local truncation error-like quantity.

dependencies.name: Runge Kutta method Taylor expansion formulas
